key_insight: A pretrained image-editing model can act as a shared solver interface for many physics problems when equations, fields, and parameters are encoded visually.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a flexible surrogate modeling route for multiphysics simulation, especially when heterogeneous PDEs and time-dependent fields must be handled with one architecture.
method: Render physical inputs and solutions as images.
Inject scalar parameters through lightweight adapters.
Train the same pretrained image model with supervision from established numerical/analytic solvers across many PDE benchmarks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation across elliptic, heat, Burgers, Ginzburg-Landau, Navier-Stokes, potential flow, elasticity, eikonal, phase-field fracture, and optimal transport tasks, plus a failed Kuramoto-Sivashinsky long-horizon test.
watch_item: Image and latent encodings can make range selection and hard physical constraints/invariants difficult to enforce, limiting reliability on chaotic or long-horizon problems.
paper_title: Image Editing Models are Numerical Solvers
